message.content: The ocean appears blue primarily due to the absorption and scattering of sunlight by the water. Sunlight is made up of various colors, and when it enters the ocean, water absorbs colors in the red part of the spectrum more efficiently than those in the blue part. As a result, blue light is scattered and reflected back to our eyes, giving the ocean its characteristic hue. Additionally, factors such as the presence of phytoplankton, sediment, and organic matter can influence the color, causing variations in
